title: Over-aggressive optimization of ORDER BY using a virtual table with IN operator
description:
drh added on 2013-04-17 23:38:35:
When a query of a virtual table uses an IN operator in the WHERE clause as
its index and it has an ORDER BY clause, it is possible that the ORDER BY
clause might be optimized away and the sort omitted when in fact the sort
is required for the correct answer.
This problem appears to have been introduced by check-in
[3d65c70343] on 2012-12-14 for version 3.7.16.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE orders(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, product TEXT NOT NULL, quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0);
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE product IN (SELECT content FROM test_table) ORDER BY quantity;
key_bug_features: ORDER BY optimization with IN operator in virtual table
